# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use a multi-stage build to keep the final image small and secure

# Stage 1: Build the web server
FROM node:14 AS web-server
WORKDIR /app/web-server
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

# Stage 2: Build the API server
FROM node:14 AS api-server
WORKDIR /app/api-server
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["node", "api.js"]

# Stage 3: Build the database
FROM postgres:13 AS db
ENV POSTGRES_DB=mydb
ENV POSTGRES_USER=myuser
ENV POSTGRES_PASSWORD=mypassword
EXPOSE 5432

# Stage 4: Build Redis
FROM redis:6.0 AS redis
EXPOSE 6379

# Final stage: Combine all services into one container
FROM alpine:latest
COPY --from=web-server /app/web-server/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
COPY --from=api-server /app/api-server/dist /usr/local/bin/api-server
COPY --from=db /var/lib/postgresql/data /var/lib/postgresql/data
COPY --from=redis /data /data

# Set up the entrypoint script to start all services
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh
ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

# Entry point script content (docker-entrypoint.sh)
#!/bin/sh
service postgresql start &
redis-server /etc/redis/redis.conf &
nginx -g "daemon off;"
